original_image_tags: A hand-drawn diagram on aged, tan-colored paper (possibly construction paper or cardstock). The diagram depicts a spatial layout of numbered points, showing distances between them in feet and inches. The lines are drawn in black ink. There are four rectangular markers numbered #1, #2, #3, and #4, and four circular areas, three of which are labeled "Burn".

elements.description: A hand-drawn diagram on a piece of aged paper. It depicts a layout with four numbered squares (#1, #2, #3, #4) and four numbered ovals (#1, #2, #3, #4). Lines connect the shapes, labeled with distances in feet and inches (e.g., 9'4", 6'4", 20', 8'6", 1'4", 6'8", 10'8"), some solid and some dashed. The ovals contain handwritten labels: #1 is 'Gray Area', #2, #3, and #4 are 'Burn'. An arrow points between ovals #2 and #3, labeled 'APPARENT DIRECTION OF BURN OR HEAT'. Some faint, illegible text is visible along the left edge.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content: a hand-drawn diagram with four numbered squares/rectangles and four numbered ovals/circular areas, distances in feet/inches, and some labels, but B adds specific quoted labels ('Gray Area', 'APPARENT DIRECTION OF BURN OR HEAT') that A does not mention, without direct contradiction.
